precedence.tieBreak: Prefer this core only when a newly acquired reusable capability changes a recorded old affordance.

scoreCriteria.rule: Full credit when one capability binds an earlier unresolved affordance to a later actionable state.
Credit only when the player can recognize the prior location without a new explanatory marker.
Scale with verified new or shortened edges caused by the capability.
Require world facts, route gates, map layers, and route simulation.

hardVetoes: No earlier visit exists
The old obstacle was never perceptible
The gate protects a fact or ending not approved by canon
The intended experience requires all traversal verbs from the opening

supports.supportedInvariant: Remembered coordinates gain new route meaning after the player obtains access to a contrasting world state.
A durable era-access capability changes affordances while landmark identity remains recognizable.

counter.challengedAssumption: Every future landmark needs an authored capability lock.
counter.boundary: When free traversal and self-authored routes are the central promise, visible terrain should invite immediate experimentation rather than deferred gating.
counter.derivedAbstention: Abstain when the target landmark is already reachable through the intended open traversal toolkit.

constraints: Acquired after the recorded first visit
Does not disclose a protected reveal
Before and after reachability are simulatable

feedback.subject: affordance
feedback.requirement: Show the capability acting on the same feature seen during the first visit.

feedback.subject: route
feedback.requirement: Keep the opened edge visible from the player's current position.

feedback.subject: location
feedback.requirement: Change only the affected affordance and its consequence; preserve landmark identity.

feedback.subject: location
feedback.requirement: Use an original confirmation cue distinct from source-pack signatures.

invariants: The first visit cannot require the later capability
The revisit must change a later action, not only decoration

revealRules: Do not name the future capability during foreshadowing
interactionWindows: Let the player test the affordance once before any hint
Return control before entering the opened route
performance.cueIntent: Make recognition of the old place precede celebration of the new capability.

topologyRequirements: One stable landmark is visible in both visits
The opened edge has a safe return edge
stage.blocking: Frame capability, old affordance, and route consequence in one readable relation.
stage.camera: A short focus cue is allowed only if the route consequence is off-screen.
navigationAssertions: First visit route is completable without the locked edge
Revisit cannot trap the player behind the new state

assertions: Capability is necessary and sufficient for the changed affordance
No source topology or object sequence survives
Before and after routes are both softlock-free

teach.task: Show one inert but distinctive affordance during a required first traversal.
teach.successEvidence: Player inspects or deliberately routes around it.
teach.failureRecovery: A later optional camera glance may repeat the feature without explaining it.

practice.task: Use the acquired capability on a safe, nearby affordance of the same class.
practice.successEvidence: Player predicts the visible state change.
practice.failureRecovery: Restore the practice object immediately and allow retry.

variation.task: Return to the old location where geometry or stakes differ from practice.
variation.successEvidence: Player identifies the remembered target without a new marker.
variation.failureRecovery: Hint references the prior observation, not the solution input.

combineOrReverse.task: Combine the capability with one story-derived prop or reverse which side of a route is accessible.
combineOrReverse.successEvidence: Player updates route planning rather than repeating one lock-key action.
combineOrReverse.failureRecovery: Keep a non-progressing exit back to the known route.

exam.task: Under narrative pressure, choose the old affordance over a tempting but ineffective new path.
exam.successEvidence: Player opens the route with no new rule introduced.
exam.failureRecovery: Checkpoint before commitment and preserve capability ownership.

runtime.fallback: If map-layer variants are unavailable, alter collision and one localized prop state while preserving the same landmark and route delta.
accessibility: Expose the unresolved affordance through shape and motion, not color alone
Offer a memory-log image substitute using original iconography
Bound revisit retry to the last safe doorway

failureModes: The capability is acquired before the first visit
The changed space is visually unrecognizable
The unlocked route has no later decision value
recovery: Preserve capability and learned affordance facts after failure
Disable the new edge if its destination cannot load

originality.retainedPrinciple: A later reusable capability changes the meaning of an earlier visible affordance.

combinationVetoes.vetoWhen: One-to-one paired coordinates, source-like transition cadence, and the same obstacle roles survive together.

precedence.tieBreak: Choose knowledge-gated only when no avatar capability, item, or world-state grant is required.

scoreCriteria.rule: Reward two or more independently readable clues that converge on one existing action.
Require proof the action could physically succeed before the player understood it.
Reward a clue order that preserves protected facts until intended discovery.
Require at least two non-spoiling hint levels.

hardVetoes: A new item, form, skill, or map layer is required
The clue depends on exact source wording
The action reveals protected canon before its gate

supports.supportedInvariant: Knowledge learned in an unsuccessful cycle changes the next route despite local state reset.
Recognition of a stable landmark turns remembered information into an actionable return destination.
A short discovered clue redirects search without granting a new movement or inventory capability.

counter.challengedAssumption: Any delayed return is a knowledge gate.
counter.boundary: A learned spell or avatar ability is a capability gate even when social memory tells the player where to use it.
counter.derivedAbstention: Reject this subtype whenever success requires a player-state capability absent during the first visit.

constraints: No clue states the input sequence verbatim

feedback.subject: knowledge-log
feedback.requirement: Record the inference in player language without certifying untested hypotheses.

feedback.subject: target-affordance
feedback.requirement: React only after the player performs the inferred action.

feedback.subject: route
feedback.requirement: Show the pre-existing geometry responding to the correctly understood action.

feedback.subject: route
feedback.requirement: Confirm success without imitating source-secret jingles.

invariants: No new capability is granted
Incorrect hypotheses produce diagnostic but non-destructive feedback

beatPositions: clue discovery after a failed or incomplete visit
silent recognition before the return action
revealRules: NPCs may restate evidence but never name the action
interactionWindows: Allow a clue-free first probe
Allow replay of every essential clue
performance.cueIntent: Make the player feel they changed, while the physical world remained available.

topologyRequirements: The same action target exists in both visits
Landmark and approach remain comparable
stage.blocking: Keep clue-bearing evidence separate from the final action target.
stage.camera: Do not focus the exact target until the player chooses to inspect it.
navigationAssertions: Incorrect action cannot consume a unique resource
Exit remains reachable after every probe

assertions: First-visit action simulation can reach the same postcondition
No clue or NPC line contains the exact solution command
Knowledge fact and world fact commit atomically

teach.task: Let the player see stable evidence and safely attempt an unexplained action.
teach.successEvidence: The failed probe produces a distinguishable reaction.

practice.task: Present a second context where a clue explains one part of the causal rule.
practice.successEvidence: Player uses evidence rather than inventory growth.
practice.failureRecovery: Replay the clue through the knowledge log.

variation.task: Change location or narrative stakes while keeping the same inference structure.
variation.successEvidence: Player transfers the rule without a direct marker.
variation.failureRecovery: Offer a relationship or landmark hint, not an input hint.

combineOrReverse.task: Combine two clues or show that an apparently new capability is irrelevant.
combineOrReverse.successEvidence: Player excludes the false capability hypothesis.
combineOrReverse.failureRecovery: Preserve all clues across checkpoint restore.

exam.task: Return to the original place and act before any explanatory performance.
exam.successEvidence: The pre-existing route opens from the inferred action.
exam.failureRecovery: Keep retry local and essential clues replayable.

runtime.fallback: If a knowledge ledger is unavailable, persist clue IDs as world facts but label them observed, inferred, or verified separately.
accessibility: Provide visual and textual equivalents for audio clues
Allow clue replay without solution disclosure
Never require exact natural-language recall

failureModes: A later item is secretly required
The route was not physically possible on first visit
recovery: Restore target state after every wrong probe
Escalate hints from evidence location to relation to action class

originality.retainedPrinciple: New understanding, not new avatar power, makes an old place actionable.

combinationVetoes.vetoWhen: The instance requires both a newly granted power and a supposedly knowledge-only solution.

precedence.tieBreak: Select as core when the state axis causally changes route reachability; comparison alone is a specialization.

scoreCriteria.rule: Reward the same axis value producing consistent consequences across terrain, blockers, and actors.
Reward meaningful tradeoffs rather than a universally better state.
Require at least two non-color channels that identify the active state.
Require a reachable state-change or escape action from every required zone.

hardVetoes: The two states are unrelated replacement maps
One state only reskins the other
The transition can irreversibly close every required route

supports.supportedInvariant: One player-controlled seasonal axis changes a small consistent set of route tiles.
Comparable geography lets causal differences across eras become route hypotheses.
Stable coordinate memory makes paired world-state route changes legible.

counter.challengedAssumption: Every directional state should compile as a discrete map-layer axis.
counter.boundary: Continuous heading changes traversal efficiency without replacing comparable route layers.
counter.derivedAbstention: Use a directional-field or vehicle contract when consequences vary continuously with heading.

constraints: At least two zones share the axis
Includes an opened and a closed or altered edge

feedback.subject: axis-wide material cues
feedback.requirement: Identify the new value before control returns.

feedback.subject: transition
feedback.requirement: Give each value an original, accessible confirmation signature.

feedback.subject: opened edge
feedback.requirement: Show the causal environmental change that permits traversal.

feedback.subject: closed edge
feedback.requirement: Animate the same rule that opened the alternative edge.

feedback.subject: state indicator
feedback.requirement: Expose the active value without relying on color.

invariants: One causal table governs every affected entity
No value dominates every objective

beatPositions: first state change in a safe zone
revealRules: Show consequences before explaining labels
interactionWindows: Let player inspect both opened and closed paths after a change
performance.cueIntent: Make the world state feel like a rule the player authors, not a scene swap.

topologyRequirements: Stable anchors survive every value
Each required zone has a legal transition or escape
stage.blocking: Place one opened and one closed edge within comparable landmark context.
stage.camera: Frame the nearest consequence, not a source-like panoramic transformation.
navigationAssertions: Every value has a route back to a transition point
Goal remains reachable in the intended sequence

assertions: Visual, collision, and interaction layers agree
Route simulator covers every axis value

teach.task: Change one safe tile class while keeping landmarks fixed.

practice.task: Use the same axis on a second entity class.

variation.task: Open one route while closing a convenient alternative.

combineOrReverse.task: Combine axis choice with a story-derived actor or resource condition.
combineOrReverse.successEvidence: Player distinguishes axis effect from the second condition.
combineOrReverse.failureRecovery: Reset only the second condition first.

exam.task: Choose and later reverse the axis value to traverse a multi-zone dependency.
exam.failureRecovery: Checkpoint before the first irreversible narrative gate.

runtime.fallback: Limit the axis to two localized zones but retain one opened and one closed edge plus reversible transition.
accessibility: Pair state with shape, motion, audio, and text labels
Allow a reduced transition animation
Provide a state-table hint after repeated route reversals

failureModes: Layer mismatch between art and collision
recovery: Atomic rollback of layer and route table
Emergency return to last transition checkpoint

originality.retainedPrinciple: One visible, reversible state axis consistently changes route affordances across stable space.

combinationVetoes.vetoWhen: The same paired coordinates, obstacle roles, transition action, and reveal order identify one source work.

precedence.tieBreak: Use only when comparing corresponding evidence is itself the puzzle verb.

scoreCriteria.rule: Reward multiple stable anchors with state-specific actionable deltas.
Reward comparisons that reveal a causal relation unavailable from one state alone.
Favor bounded transition cost that permits deliberate back-and-forth testing.
Require distinct, accessible state cues without replacing landmark identity.

hardVetoes: States share no stable anchors
One state only replaces narrative dressing
Comparison never changes player action

supports.supportedInvariant: Coordinate correspondence turns remembered geography into a comparison hypothesis.
Stable geography and selected causal differences let the player compare rather than search unrelated maps.

counter.boundary: A single configuration that changes team height has no corresponding world states or stable anchor pairs to compare.
counter.derivedAbstention: Reject when the mechanic changes only actor configuration inside one unchanged state.

constraints: Exactly two comparison states for this application
At least one actionable causal delta
Not stated directly by a cue

feedback.subject: stable anchors
feedback.requirement: Preserve silhouette and screen relation while state-specific details change.

feedback.subject: active state
feedback.requirement: Label the active state with text and non-color icon shape.

feedback.subject: comparison log
feedback.requirement: Record observed differences separately from inferred cause.

feedback.subject: anchor delta
feedback.requirement: Frame the affected anchor for less than the configured cue budget.

feedback.subject: causal result
feedback.requirement: Show the delta acting, not a source-like transformation montage.

invariants: State identity and anchor identity are distinct facts
At least one player action follows from comparison

beatPositions: first contrast after each state is independently legible
payoff after player commits an inference
revealRules: Cues may show differences but cannot state causal interpretation
interactionWindows: Allow switching twice before a hint
Return agency with target anchor in view
performance.cueIntent: Let comparison produce the revelation rather than narrating it.

topologyRequirements: Anchor coordinates or adjacency remain comparable
State-specific routes may differ without erasing orientation
stage.blocking: Place corresponding anchors within similar approach context.
stage.camera: Use matching framing only for original, non-source-specific anchors.
navigationAssertions: Both states can reach a comparison anchor
Transition never strands the player

assertions: Anchor semantic IDs match across states

teach.task: Present one anchor in two states with a single obvious delta.
teach.successEvidence: Player identifies what stayed constant and what changed.

practice.task: Ask the player to use a second anchor to confirm the same relation.
practice.failureRecovery: Highlight anchor identity, not the causal answer.

variation.task: Change which sensory channel exposes the delta.
variation.successEvidence: Player retains the causal relation across visual and behavioral evidence.

combineOrReverse.task: Make an action in one state alter evidence in the other.
combineOrReverse.failureRecovery: Rollback the action while preserving observations.

exam.task: Choose the correct state and anchor from memory under route pressure.
exam.successEvidence: Player acts on the inferred relation without a new clue.

runtime.fallback: Use two small corresponding rooms and a transition portal while retaining stable anchor IDs and actionable deltas.
accessibility: Describe state deltas textually
Use distinct motion and audio as well as palette
Allow slower matching transitions and comparison-log replay

failureModes: Anchor identity changes with surface art

originality.retainedPrinciple: Stable anchors let differences between states become actionable information.
forbiddenTransfers: source light/dark or past/future icon sets

combinationVetoes.vetoWhen: The combination preserves a source map pairing, transition ritual, object-role sequence, and narrative purpose.

precedence.tieBreak: Select only as a support when the core pattern already provides the future action or reward.

scoreCriteria.rule: Reward enough delay for memory but not enough to erase context.
Reward repeated optional glimpses from distinct routes.
Require original silhouette readable without color.
Require later use to change route, knowledge, or relationship choice.

hardVetoes: Landmark is first shown only at payoff
Its identity copies source silhouette or staging
Later use has no player action

supports.supportedInvariant: A visible destination can create a self-authored route before its contents are known.
Known coordinates and vertical landmarks gain later utility from a new approach layer.

counter.challengedAssumption: Every memorable spatial gate is a future-use landmark.
counter.boundary: A temporary multiplayer chokepoint coordinates an immediate reunion but does not promise a delayed future action.
counter.derivedAbstention: Reject if sighting and use occur in the same local coordination beat.

constraints: Changes route, knowledge, or relationship state

feedback.subject: landmark
feedback.requirement: Hold a navigable sightline long enough for recognition without forced zoom.

feedback.subject: landmark
feedback.requirement: Reframe from a player-known approach, not a copied reveal composition.

feedback.subject: payoff route
feedback.requirement: Expose the resulting choice from the landmark itself.

invariants: First sighting does not require interaction
Payoff requires player action

revealRules: Do not label future function at first sighting
interactionWindows: Player may ignore or mark the first sighting
Control returns before payoff action
performance.cueIntent: Reward remembered place identity rather than surprise through spectacle alone.

topologyRequirements: At least one early sightline
Silhouette remains readable from payoff approach
stage.blocking: Keep foreground clutter from erasing the landmark outline.
stage.camera: No forced camera if the player already looks at it.
navigationAssertions: Ignoring the first sighting cannot block required progress
Payoff path has a safe exit

teach.task: Place the landmark on a safe route where it can be seen but not used.
teach.successEvidence: Player looks, marks, or routes with it.

practice.task: Use the landmark as a navigation reference before its main payoff.
practice.successEvidence: Player reaches a nearby objective by sightline.
practice.failureRecovery: Map label appears only after discovery.

variation.task: Show the same silhouette from a new elevation or state.
variation.successEvidence: Player recognizes identity despite changed context.
variation.failureRecovery: Use original shape and audio cue together.

combineOrReverse.task: Let a capability, fact, or relationship change what the landmark permits.
combineOrReverse.successEvidence: Player connects prior memory to the new condition.
combineOrReverse.failureRecovery: Keep alternate route back to observation point.

exam.task: Require choosing the remembered landmark among multiple visible destinations.
exam.successEvidence: Correct approach leads to the promised later action.
exam.failureRecovery: Wrong destinations yield bounded detours, not failure.

runtime.fallback: Use repeated map-edge glimpses and a persistent original icon if long-distance rendering is unavailable.
accessibility: Make silhouette readable at high contrast
Offer optional non-spoiling map marks
Pair visual identity with unique original ambient sound

failureModes: First sighting is a forced spoiler
Payoff replaces recognition with a new visual identity

originality.retainedPrinciple: A distinctive place seen early becomes meaningful when the player can act there later.
forbiddenTransfers: source towers, temples, castles, or mountain silhouettes
source arrival music and camera moves

combinationVetoes.vetoWhen: Landmark silhouette, approach graph, reward role, and reveal staging converge on one source destination.

precedence.tieBreak: Select as core only when one rule can sustain all five authored stages.

scoreCriteria.rule: Every exam prerequisite must point to a prior teach, practice, and variation event.
Reward distinct feedback for wrong target, wrong timing, and correct application.
Reward meaningful context change without changing the invariant.
Require bounded retries at teach and exam stages.

hardVetoes: The exam introduces an unpracticed rule
The rule appears fewer than five times
Systemic discovery is the intended experience and authored ordering would reduce agency

supports.supportedInvariant: A bounded challenge foreshadows a tool, confirms it, varies it, and uses it in later dependencies.
A durable tool is introduced through a safe local example before larger spatial and encounter applications.
One action rule escalates through deliberate variations inside a bounded structure.
A previously practiced tool rule becomes a readable boss-scale exam.

counter.challengedAssumption: Every portable causal rule needs a fixed authored lesson sequence.
counter.boundary: A systemic material relation can be learned in many player-authored contexts and should not be forced into a linear five-stage arc.
counter.derivedAbstention: Abstain when solution order and discovery context are intentionally open and no canonical exam exists.

feedback.subject: causal target
feedback.requirement: Show the exact state transition that confirms the invariant.

feedback.subject: lesson log
feedback.requirement: Record the rule in player-facing terms only after successful action.

feedback.subject: next lesson zone
feedback.requirement: Open a route whose visible context previews the next variation.

feedback.subject: exam variables
feedback.requirement: Frame only variables already present in the lesson ledger.

feedback.subject: exam pressure
feedback.requirement: Increase urgency without masking diagnostic rule feedback.

invariants: One ruleRef persists through all stages
Exam has no new required variable

beatPositions: exam after at least one variation and combination
revealRules: Feedback may name result but not future application
interactionWindows: Allow voluntary repetition at teach and practice
Return control before exam starts
performance.cueIntent: Make growing competence visible through changed contexts rather than longer explanations.

topologyRequirements: Teach and practice are adjacent enough for recall
Exam has a local checkpoint and escape
stage.blocking: Expose one new variable per stage until combination.
stage.camera: Keep cause and effect within one frame at teach; widen only after mastery.
navigationAssertions: Skipping optional repetition cannot skip required rule evidence
Exam route remains retryable

assertions: Every exam ruleRef appears in prior ledger
Retry cost stays under configured bound

teach.task: Isolate one causal variable with no meaningful failure penalty.
teach.successEvidence: Player causes and sees the intended state change.

practice.task: Require the same action twice with player-controlled timing.
practice.successEvidence: Second success occurs without a direct prompt.

variation.task: Change geometry, actor, or goal while preserving the causal invariant.
variation.successEvidence: Player selects the rule despite changed surface context.

combineOrReverse.task: Combine with one previously mastered rule or reverse the state direction.
combineOrReverse.successEvidence: Player sequences both known operations correctly.

exam.task: Apply the rule under narrative pressure with no new hidden dependency.
exam.successEvidence: Lesson ledger proves every required inference was practiced.
exam.failureRecovery: Restore exam-local state and retain mastery ledger.

runtime.fallback: Compress practice and variation into one room only if both attempts remain player-controlled and separately logged.
accessibility: Offer repeatable demonstrations after a failed attempt
Separate timing difficulty from rule comprehension
Allow extended exam windows without changing the rule

recovery: Provide one-step hint tied to the last failed hypothesis

originality.retainedPrinciple: A rule becomes mastery through safe introduction, repetition, context change, combination, and a fair exam.

combinationVetoes.vetoWhen: Opening beats, tool order, room graph, and exam staging converge on one source dungeon.

precedence.tieBreak: Use as core when multiple causal paths are the experience goal; object composition may support one path family.

scoreCriteria.rule: Reward paths that use different verbs or object roles before converging.
Require one observable state invariant independent of path.
Reject a nominal alternative that is dominated in every cost dimension.
Require automated reachability for each declared path and rollback.

hardVetoes: Canon requires one exact action sequence
Only one path survives resource constraints
An alternative bypasses the story reveal gate

supports.supportedInvariant: A narrow material rule can support different plans with independently testable consequences.
Legible constraints and available parts support locally authored solution paths.
A growing functional vocabulary can satisfy one problem state through different callable roles.

counter.challengedAssumption: Every expressive verb implies multiple valid solutions.
counter.boundary: A repeatedly invoked time verb can remain the single required authored action for a gate.
counter.derivedAbstention: Reject when every valid route must invoke the same verb in the same causal position.

constraints: Restores consumables and movable objects consistently

feedback.subject: shared outcome
feedback.requirement: Show the same world result regardless of chosen path.

feedback.subject: path-specific contribution
feedback.requirement: Acknowledge the player's method without ranking one as canonical.

feedback.subject: converged route
feedback.requirement: Expose the same next goal while leaving path-specific aftermath visible.

invariants: At least two non-dominated paths exist
All paths preserve reveal order

revealRules: Characters react to outcome and optionally method, never declare one method correct
interactionWindows: Allow abandoning an uncommitted path
Do not interrupt construction or experimentation with solution text
performance.cueIntent: Celebrate causal authorship while keeping the story outcome coherent.

topologyRequirements: Each path has legal placement and movement space
Paths converge before protected story content
stage.blocking: Keep the goal state visible from more than one approach.
stage.camera: Follow the player's chosen method rather than a pre-authored source solution.
navigationAssertions: Each declared path reaches outcome
Rollback restores all path-local blockers

teach.task: Show two verbs affecting the same low-risk state variable.
teach.successEvidence: Player observes both as valid causes.

practice.task: Offer a choice between two simple paths to one result.
practice.successEvidence: Either path commits the same outcome.

variation.task: Change resource distribution so the previously chosen path is less convenient.

combineOrReverse.task: Allow a hybrid path or require reversing an intermediate state.
combineOrReverse.successEvidence: Graph validator accepts the novel sequence.

exam.task: Reach one canon-safe outcome with no authored method marker.
exam.successEvidence: Chosen path is valid, non-dominated, and comprehensible.

runtime.fallback: Ship two explicitly authored but mechanically distinct paths if free composition cannot be simulated deterministically.
accessibility: Provide at least one low-dexterity path
Expose resource and placement constraints before commitment
Allow full problem reset without narrative penalty

originality.retainedPrinciple: Several causally distinct paths can satisfy one explicit world-state invariant.
forbiddenTransfers: source shrine or rift solution layouts

combinationVetoes.vetoWhen: Object roles, part arrangement, action order, and outcome reproduce a known source solution.

precedence.tieBreak: Select only when bounded object roles add a distinct story-fit function to an independently valid core.

scoreCriteria.rule: Reward roles that change different state dimensions.
Require each role to be readable before placement.
Reward combinations that produce emergent but bounded consequences.
Require deterministic reset for invalid placement and consumption.

hardVetoes: Only actor formation changes
A source-specific exact recipe is required
One role solves every object problem

supports.supportedInvariant: Parts with legible physical constraints produce local solutions through placement.
Objects and actors become callable functional roles that can be positioned for different needs.

counter.challengedAssumption: Any configurable geometry is object-role composition.
counter.boundary: A formation changes several player-controlled actors as one verb; it does not create independently placeable objects with composable roles.
counter.derivedAbstention: Reject when all components are avatar bodies and cannot persist as independent world objects.

feedback.subject: role catalog
feedback.requirement: Show function and cost with original symbols.

feedback.subject: placement preview
feedback.requirement: Differentiate legal, unstable, and blocking placement.

feedback.subject: placement
feedback.requirement: Optional confirmation with non-haptic equivalent.

feedback.subject: role interaction
feedback.requirement: Show which role caused each state change.

feedback.subject: composition result
feedback.requirement: Use layered original cues that preserve causal order.

feedback.subject: resource count
feedback.requirement: Preview consumption before commit.

invariants: Every result derives from declared roles
Invalid placement cannot consume unique resources

revealRules: Do not show a canonical recipe
interactionWindows: Allow preview and cancel
Keep result inspectable before next cue
performance.cueIntent: Make object function readable without turning the system into a copied catalog.

topologyRequirements: Placement zones include escape cells
Objects cannot seal all exits
stage.blocking: Keep active roles and affected targets in readable proximity.
stage.camera: Frame the causal chain, not a predesigned recipe angle.
navigationAssertions: Every placement state has reset path
Required objects remain recoverable

assertions: No object role or silhouette copies a source asset

teach.task: Demonstrate one object role on one target with safe placement preview.

practice.task: Place the same role in two legal positions with different spatial outcomes.

variation.successEvidence: Player chooses role by target state.

combineOrReverse.task: Compose two roles or reverse one result through a declared relation.

exam.task: Reach a goal using a legal composition not demonstrated verbatim.
exam.successEvidence: Table validates the composition and exits remain open.

runtime.fallback: Use three fixed role-bearing props and snap points instead of free placement while retaining at least two compositions.
accessibility: Preview role and consumption in text
Offer snap placement and rotation alternatives
Provide undo and full reset

originality.retainedPrinciple: A small, legible role algebra lets placed objects create bounded causal combinations.
forbiddenTransfers: source placement UI and power costs

combinationVetoes.vetoWhen: Object roles, exact arrangement, action order, and payoff identify a source construction or copied-object puzzle.

precedence.tieBreak: Reject if route constraint removes the story's core exploration promise.

scoreCriteria.rule: Reward meaningful route, hazard, timing, or disembark choices.
Require landmarks that support planning before each branch.
Reward active control without constant precision burden.
Require safe stop, reroute, and checkpoint recovery.

hardVetoes: Open-direction traversal is the central fantasy
Travel is mostly noninteractive
Missed branch requires long unskippable repetition

supports.supportedInvariant: A heading-bound vehicle turns distant landmarks into route planning and optional detours.
Added graph edges make vehicle progression and return planning explicit.

counter.challengedAssumption: A route-bound vehicle always improves landmark exploration.
counter.boundary: Free climbing and self-authored approach are incompatible with a rail or lane when unrestricted route authorship is the experience promise.
counter.derivedAbstention: Abstain when the player is meant to invent terrain routes rather than choose within a network.

feedback.subject: heading and next branch
feedback.requirement: Expose next legal decisions before commitment.

feedback.subject: vehicle motion
feedback.requirement: Match speed to route state and accessibility setting.

feedback.subject: network map
feedback.requirement: Add the edge without auto-selecting a destination.

feedback.subject: physical route
feedback.requirement: Show the story-derived reason the edge opened.

feedback.subject: branch landmark
feedback.requirement: Frame alternatives without taking steering control.

feedback.subject: hazard or stop
feedback.requirement: Use distinct original cues with captions.

invariants: Every branch is previewed
Player retains a meaningful input during travel

beatPositions: arrival cue after player regains local movement
revealRules: Do not expose unvisited destination facts through the route map
interactionWindows: Steering remains active through cues
Allow safe stop before irreversible narrative arrival
performance.cueIntent: Turn travel into readable anticipation rather than passive spectacle.

topologyRequirements: Network has alternate or return edges
Stops connect to local walkable spaces
stage.blocking: Keep hazards and branch landmarks inside travel sightline.
stage.camera: No source-like ocean or railway composition; camera serves current choice distance.
navigationAssertions: Missed branch reaches a reroute stop
Unlocked edges never strand vehicle

teach.task: Drive one short segment with one safe stop and one visible landmark.
teach.successEvidence: Player can stop and resume deliberately.

variation.task: Add a hazard or timing window without hiding route choice.
variation.successEvidence: Player manages movement and planning together.
variation.failureRecovery: Reduce speed and restore at prior stop.

combineOrReverse.task: Unlock a shortcut or reverse travel direction using a story-derived condition.

exam.task: Navigate branches, hazard, and disembark choice under narrative pressure.
exam.successEvidence: Correct destination reached through player decisions.

runtime.fallback: Use a node-and-segment travel interface with active branch and hazard decisions if continuous vehicle physics is unavailable.
accessibility: Adjust travel speed and decision preview distance
Caption navigation audio
Offer auto-steer while preserving branch choice

recovery: Restore vehicle and hazard state atomically

originality.retainedPrinciple: A constrained travel network remains engaging through branches, hazards, timing, and local stops.
forbiddenTransfers: source sea grids or rail layouts
source boats, trains, charts, or track UI

combinationVetoes.vetoWhen: Vehicle form, macro network, landmark rhythm, and arrival structure identify one source voyage.

precedence.tieBreak: Use as core when one acquisition transaction commits persistent state that improves at least two distinct future-usable functional dimensions; otherwise use the matching landmark, capability, recovery, or stage-role pattern.

scoreCriteria.rule: Require at least two distinct functional dimensions, not duplicated labels for one function.
Require every declared function state to be committed atomically by the same acquisition transaction and preserved across its future-use boundary.
Require a typed before and after comparison for each future-use context.
Require legible, original feedback for acquisition and every declared function.

hardVetoes: The payoff is only score, collection percentage, currency, or one immediate value spike
No acquisition transaction commits one or more persistent state refs
Fewer than two distinct functional dimensions are bound
Any function lacks its persistent-state, future-use-context, feedback, or before-and-after actionability binding
A function copies a source reward object, site, function bundle, or feedback cadence
The payoff invalidates protected story order

supports.supportedInvariant: Completing an exploration detour commits persistent travel-anchor and growth states whose mobility or topology and capacity functions expand later actionability.
Activating an exploration landmark commits persistent illumination, map, and recovery state whose perception or topology and recovery functions improve later exploration.

counter.challengedAssumption: Every role-changing reward establishes a persistent multi-function payoff.
counter.boundary: A replay material that changes one bounded stage role does not by itself establish persistent multi-function state across a revisitable world.
counter.derivedAbstention: Use a stage-loadout or role-reward contract when the payoff has only one functional dimension or exists only inside fixed course sessions.

constraints: Commits the complete reward state set atomically
May follow optional or required exploration; optionality is not assumed
Contains one or more persistent state refs committed by the acquisition transaction
Every ref survives its declared save, travel, revisit, or future-use boundary
A bundle may use several state refs; one indivisible scalar ref is not required
Contains at least two function refs in distinct functional dimensions
Every function cites a member persistentStateRef, a futureUseContextRef, a feedbackRef, and actionabilityDelta.before plus actionabilityDelta.after
Every after value is more actionable than its before value
Duplicated labels for one dimension do not count twice
Protected story order and functional balance bounds are defined

feedback.subject: persistent reward state
feedback.requirement: Confirm the original payoff category without claiming optionality, branch count, or a source-shaped solution.

feedback.subject: acquisition transaction
feedback.requirement: Make the atomic commit legible without copying a source reward site or reveal cadence.

restores: all acquisition-local state when commit fails before the gate
preserves: the complete persistent state set after a successful atomic commit

feedback.subject: future-use context
feedback.requirement: Expose each function's changed actionability only when its bound future-use context is known or reached.

feedback.subject: function feedback
feedback.requirement: Use each function's feedbackRef to distinguish the before and after state accessibly.

preserves: the atomically committed persistent state set

invariants: One acquisition transaction commits all declared persistent state refs atomically
At least two functions occupy distinct functional dimensions
Every function cites its persistentStateRef, futureUseContextRef, feedbackRef, and before-and-after actionability delta
Reward does not overwrite protected order

beatPositions: acquisition after the bound exploration action
recognition when each bound future-use context becomes available
revealRules: Explain functional category without revealing an exact future solution
Do not imply that acquisition was optional when the story model does not say so
interactionWindows: Let the player observe acquisition-state feedback
Provide an accessible before-and-after cue at every function's future-use context
performance.cueIntent: Make one persistent payoff read as broader future agency through distinct, testable functions rather than as score or branch count.

topologyRequirements: Every declared future-use context has a reachable or actionable fixture
Each context reads a committed persistentStateRef
Save and reload reconstruct derived function availability
stage.blocking: Place function feedback at its bound future-use context, not at an inferred unselected branch.
stage.camera: Focus the original state change and current function rather than a source-like reward silhouette or site.
navigationAssertions: Every declared function has a measured before and after actionability state
Loss of one context cannot corrupt the committed reward state set
Rollback or interrupted acquisition restores one coherent pre-commit or post-commit state

assertions: One or more persistent state refs are committed by the acquisition transaction
At least two distinct function refs each close persistentStateRef, futureUseContextRef, feedbackRef, and before-and-after actionabilityDelta
Immediate score, single capability, landmark-only, recovery-only, and single-stage-role fixtures abstain
No optionality, non-domination, unselected-choice, or copied source bundle claim survives

teach.task: Commit the reward transaction and demonstrate one safe function with explicit persistent-state feedback.
teach.successEvidence: The player can identify one changed actionability state and its feedback channel.
teach.failureRecovery: Before commit, restore the entire acquisition transaction; after commit, preserve the complete state set.

practice.task: Present a later-use context in a second functional dimension.
practice.successEvidence: The second function reads a committed state ref and its after-state is more actionable than its before-state.
practice.failureRecovery: Keep the persistent state set and allow the context to be retried.

variation.task: Present another context that changes function surface without duplicating a functional dimension.
variation.successEvidence: The player transfers the payoff's broader value without a copied source pairing.
variation.failureRecovery: Hint the functional dimension and feedback channel only.

combineOrReverse.task: Combine a declared function with an existing verb or temporarily suppress one context without deleting persistent state.
combineOrReverse.successEvidence: Derived availability changes coherently while the acquisition transaction remains committed.
combineOrReverse.failureRecovery: Reconstruct all function bindings from persistent state on load or rollback.

exam.task: Use at least two distinct functions in separate future-use contexts after save or travel.
exam.successEvidence: Both contexts show positive before-and-after actionability deltas from the same acquisition transaction's state set.
exam.failureRecovery: Restore the last coherent checkpoint and re-derive function availability.

runtime.fallback: Use an atomic set of durable world, knowledge, relationship, map, inventory, or recovery facts; do not collapse a multi-state bundle into one inferred scalar ref.
accessibility: Explain every functional dimension in plain text
Provide visual and textual equivalents for each feedbackRef
Never encode actionability deltas by color alone
Avoid precision-only reward utility

failureModes: Only one functional dimension is present
Duplicated labels masquerade as separate functions
The acquisition transaction partially commits its state set
A future-use context or feedback ref is missing
A source reward site and function bundle are copied
recovery: Atomically roll back the complete acquisition transaction before commit
Preserve the complete persistent state set after commit
Reconstruct derived function availability on load
Keep every bound future-use context retryable or diagnosable

originality.retainedPrinciple: An exploration payoff expands later possibility when its persistent state improves at least two distinct functional dimensions, such as traversal, capacity, information, perception, topology, preparation, relationship access, or recovery, instead of granting only immediate score or value.
forbiddenTransfers: source reward objects, silhouettes, names, or acquisition staging
source shrine, cave, root, tower, chest, or map-node composition
source health-growth, fast-travel, mapping, illumination, or recovery pairing
source UI, sound, reveal cadence, economy, or exact function bundle

combinationVetoes.vetoWhen: Acquisition site, reward-state type, function bundle, future-use contexts, and feedback cadence converge on one source reward loop.

precedence.tieBreak: Select only after optionality and required-route reachability are proven, and only when a readable clue bounds the test target; a required capability gate for the same affordance takes precedence.

scoreCriteria.rule: Require at least one fiction-valid visual, audio, spatial-relation, or authored-record clue.
Require the clue to bound the target set before the test action is attempted.
Require the complete main route to remain valid when the optional goal is ignored.
Require successful feedback to identify the tested target and changed optional state without giving a walkthrough solution.

hardVetoes: Secret is mandatory progression in disguise
Only exhaustive testing of visually identical targets can reveal it
Cue and solution reproduce a source landmark-object pair

supports.supportedInvariant: A persistent photograph clue bounds a location-matching test and the optional recovered-memory scene confirms the match.
An activation scene plus map or visual signal bounds a linked aerial target and the optional chest response confirms the cross-region relation.

counter.challengedAssumption: Every hidden terrain response is a readable optional secret.
counter.boundary: A tool revealing terrain is insufficient when the outcome is required or compatible targets are not distinguished by a learnable clue.
counter.derivedAbstention: Use a capability gate or general hidden-state interaction when optionality or clue-bounded inference cannot be demonstrated.

constraints: Required route and protected reveals remain completable when unclaimed
At least one visual, audio, spatial-relation, or authored-record clue
Available when the clue is readable
Does not carry a required canon reveal

feedback.subject: clue legibility
feedback.requirement: Make at least one clue repeatable and distinguishable without an objective marker.

feedback.subject: cue equivalence
feedback.requirement: Provide shape, sound, or text equivalents for every essential clue.

feedback.subject: successful causal response
feedback.requirement: On success, identify the tested target and changed optional state through the bound feedback reference.

feedback.subject: causal reveal
feedback.requirement: Frame the exact tested target and changed optional state before the reward.

feedback.subject: optional reward
feedback.requirement: Describe its value without marking the optional goal as required completion.

invariants: Main route never depends on the optional goal
Wrong tests cannot consume irreplaceable required resources

revealRules: Never announce the complete solution
At least one clue must remain available before testing
interactionWindows: Allow clue inspection without triggering
Return control between successful response and reward collection
performance.cueIntent: Make the player feel observant rather than lucky.

topologyRequirements: Optional target lies off the critical path
At least one clue is readable from a safe approach
The clue bounds a finite target set
Return route remains available
stage.blocking: Do not hide the only clue behind the action it teaches.
stage.camera: Show the clue and its bounded test relation without a walkthrough prompt.
navigationAssertions: Skip route reaches every required goal
Failure route returns to a safe junction

assertions: At least one registered clue precedes the bounded test
Success identifies the tested target and changed optional state

teach.task: Place a harmless anomaly beside a familiar optional reward and let one available action produce a small response.
teach.successEvidence: Player associates cue grammar with testability.
teach.failureRecovery: Repeat the cue from a safer angle without marking the answer.

practice.task: Use the same cue grammar at a noncritical side site with a clear local reward.
practice.successEvidence: Player tests deliberately rather than sweeping every surface.
practice.failureRecovery: Wrong action gives categorized negative feedback.

variation.task: Split the clue across two sensory or spatial observations.
variation.failureRecovery: A nearby NPC or journal restates one property, not the solution.

combineOrReverse.task: Require a known action in an unexpected context or a two-step action order.
combineOrReverse.successEvidence: Player transfers the rule without source-shaped iconography.
combineOrReverse.failureRecovery: Partial success persists and exposes the missing step.

exam.task: Offer a fully optional site whose cues, cost, and reward must be evaluated without a quest marker.
exam.successEvidence: Player can discover, solve, or consciously skip it.
exam.failureRecovery: Site remains revisitable and spent test resources are refundable.

runtime.fallback: Use one authored-record clue and a deterministic inspect-then-act interaction when environmental cue fidelity is limited.
accessibility: Provide shape or text equivalents for color cues
Caption directional and reactive sounds
Allow extended inspection time
Offer optional clue recap without exposing the action

failureModes: Feedback makes every surface look suspect
recovery: Keep site revisitable after chapter transitions

originality.retainedPrinciple: Optional content is readable when at least one observable clue supports a bounded player test and successful testing gives unmistakable feedback that confirms the same clue-action relation; abstain when discovery requires blind exhaustive testing or when the route is required.
forbiddenTransfers: source secret chimes, silhouettes, and reward rooms

combinationVetoes.vetoWhen: Clue surface, required action, hidden-room topology, and reward role collectively identify one source secret.

precedence.tieBreak: Select when map state and at least one situated NPC state must remain consistent through one authoritative fact; use embodied reveal when the transition presentation itself is primary.

scoreCriteria.rule: Require the map and NPC delta to read the same authoritative world fact.
Reward dialogue, placement, schedule, service, tactic, relationship, or route-access changes beyond a flavor-only bark.
Require a visible world-state delta alongside the NPC delta.
Require before, after, save/resume, and rollback states to keep map and NPC reads aligned.

hardVetoes: Map and NPC use different fact keys
Reaction is only a flavor bark with no NPC-state delta
NPC-only quest progress has no visible world-state delta

supports.supportedInvariant: One governing province state changes lighting, routes, inhabitant availability, services, and renewed interactions coherently.
The active era is legible through coordinated lighting, occupancy, geometry, music, dialogue, resident availability, and path changes.

counter.challengedAssumption: Every visible world change implies an NPC-state reaction.
counter.boundary: A visible environmental response with no actor state reading that fact is not this pattern.
counter.derivedAbstention: Abstain when no NPC dialogue, availability, behavior, service, relationship, schedule, or access value changes.

constraints: Map and NPC states read this exact reference
Visible before and after states are defined
Situated in or affected by the changed state
Differs observably in dialogue, placement, schedule, service, tactic, relationship, or access

feedback.subject: visible world delta
feedback.requirement: Expose the changed world state before or alongside the NPC-state delta.

feedback.subject: observable NPC delta
feedback.requirement: Expose at least one changed dialogue, placement, schedule, service, tactic, relationship, or route-access value.

stateWrites: NPC dialogue, placement, schedule, service, tactic, relationship, or access state
invariants: Map and NPC states read one fact key
At least one NPC-state delta is observable

revealRules: Do not require the NPC to name the cause
Do not require personal-stakes interpretation
Do not duplicate the world fact as an unlinked dialogue flag
interactionWindows: NPC-state deltas may be observed through dialogue, placement, availability, behavior, service, or access
performance.cueIntent: Keep character state consistent with the world state the player can observe.

topologyRequirements: At least one affected NPC state remains observable after the change
Visible world and NPC deltas can be compared
Exit and recovery path remain open
stage.blocking: Stage the NPC delta where the same world state is legible.
stage.camera: Frame actor and changed local condition together when spatially possible.
navigationAssertions: Post-change NPC route or availability is valid
Rollback restores a coherent before state

assertions: Map and NPC use the same fact reference
At least one NPC state changes observably
Before, after, save/resume, and rollback states never disagree

teach.task: Bind one small reversible world delta and one nearby NPC-state delta to the same fact.
teach.successEvidence: Player sees that the map and NPC state agree.
teach.failureRecovery: Restore both states from the same checkpoint fact.

practice.task: Reuse the shared fact for a changed dialogue, placement, service, or access value.
practice.successEvidence: Player predicts the NPC delta from the visible world state.
practice.failureRecovery: Keep one neutral observation route available.

variation.task: Express the NPC delta through a different state channel without changing the authoritative fact.
variation.successEvidence: Player recognizes consistency across dialogue, behavior, availability, or access.
variation.failureRecovery: Expose a second nonverbal or text-equivalent cue.

combineOrReverse.task: Rollback or reselect the world state and require both map and NPC states to follow the same fact.
combineOrReverse.successEvidence: No subsystem retains a contradictory state.
combineOrReverse.failureRecovery: Recompute both outputs from the authoritative fact.

exam.task: Save, resume, and rollback a changed district while comparing visible world and NPC states.
exam.successEvidence: Every route preserves shared-fact identity and at least one observable NPC delta.
exam.failureRecovery: Restore the last coherent world-fact checkpoint.

runtime.fallback: Keep actors stationary but condition pose, prop, dialogue, and service access on the same verified world fact as the visible map state.
accessibility: Summarize changed actor availability in the journal
Do not require reading subtle facial animation alone
Provide nonverbal or text-equivalent state cues
Allow repeated non-advancing state summaries

failureModes: Map and NPC read different flags
Flavor-only bark is mistaken for a state delta
recovery: Recompute map and NPC states from the authoritative fact
Restore the previous NPC state on rollback
Provide a neutral access alternative when a social route closes

originality.retainedPrinciple: Use one authoritative world fact to drive both a visible world-state delta and at least one situated NPC dialogue, availability, behavior, service, or access delta, so character response cannot contradict the state the player can observe.
forbiddenTransfers: source town schedules and dialogue beats

combinationVetoes.vetoWhen: Affected cast, regional before/after structure, restoration action, and reaction sequence identify one source campaign beat.

precedence.tieBreak: Select when an atomic world-state transition and its coordinated before-and-after cue are the primary payoff; select route or NPC patterns when topology or social-state consistency is primary.

scoreCriteria.rule: Require one transaction to commit or roll back every affected state.
Require player-readable completion, transformation, and after-state consequence.
Require a performance cue that preserves the relation between the before and after variants.
Require at least one affected navigation, actor, or interaction state to remain observable in subsequent play.

hardVetoes: Affected subsystems commit from different fact identities
Only a door, object animation, or treasure container changes
No navigation, actor, or interaction consequence is observable after the cue
Transition copies a source milestone staging chain

supports.supportedInvariant: Completing a province restoration produces a coordinated visual and musical transition plus persistent inhabitant, service, movement, and route changes.
Completing a rift expedition produces a resolution cue plus restored world elements, narrative access, conversations, rewards, and onward routes.

counter.challengedAssumption: Every visible environmental response is an atomic world-state transition.
counter.boundary: A local terrain response or pickup does not establish a coordinated world variant with an observable navigation, actor, or interaction consequence.
counter.derivedAbstention: Use an environmental interaction or reward-feedback pattern when no world variant and no subsequent changed-state consequence exist.

constraints: Atomic commit and rollback policy is defined
Skip and replay preserve the same committed state
At least one navigation, actor, or interaction delta
Every affected state reads the transition fact
At least one delta is observable in subsequent play

feedback.subject: coordinated after state
feedback.requirement: Expose the changed variant and at least one affected state as consequences of the completed transition.

feedback.subject: before-to-after relation
feedback.requirement: Preserve spatial orientation or another stable comparison anchor across the coordinated cue.

feedback.subject: transition equivalence
feedback.requirement: Caption sound cues and provide reduced-motion presentation without changing the committed after state.

invariants: Transition commits atomically
Every affected state reads the transition fact
At least one changed state remains observable in subsequent play

beatPositions: subsequent play exposes a changed state
revealRules: A protected reveal is conditional, not universal
Affected actors are shown only when the transition changes them
Cue skip and replay preserve the committed after state
interactionWindows: The completion action precedes the cue
No maximum return-to-control time is asserted by this evidence contract
performance.cueIntent: Make one completed state change legible across its affected world systems.

topologyRequirements: A before-to-after comparison anchor is available
At least one navigation, actor, or interaction delta remains observable
Changed route graph has a safe recovery node when navigation changes
stage.blocking: Include an actor only when that actor is affected by the transition.
stage.camera: Maintain spatial orientation or an equivalent readable comparison across the cue.
navigationAssertions: Required recovery node is reachable after transition
Old-state-only route cannot strand the player mid-transaction

assertions: At least one affected state is observable in subsequent play
Skip and save/resume preserve the same after state
Every interrupted transition rolls back coherently

teach.task: Use a small reversible world-state change with a clear before-to-after cue and one later observable consequence.
teach.successEvidence: Player identifies the completed transition and changed state.
teach.failureRecovery: Replay the cue from the same comparison anchor.

practice.task: Coordinate a map-state change with one navigation, actor, or interaction delta.
practice.successEvidence: Player connects the cue to the persistent consequence.
practice.failureRecovery: Expose the changed state again through a safe route.

variation.task: Change a different subsystem while preserving the same atomic transition contract.
variation.successEvidence: Player recognizes the shared state change without requiring an actor or protected reveal.
variation.failureRecovery: Provide a replayable factual before-and-after recap.

combineOrReverse.task: Combine several affected states in one transaction or roll all of them back together.
combineOrReverse.successEvidence: No subsystem displays a mixed before-and-after state.
combineOrReverse.failureRecovery: Checkpoint restores the complete before variant.

exam.task: Commit, skip, save, resume, and rollback a world transition with a coordinated cue and observable later consequence.
exam.successEvidence: Every route preserves one fact identity and a coherent world variant.
exam.failureRecovery: Restore the last coherent transition checkpoint.

runtime.fallback: Swap deterministic before and after variants at a stable comparison anchor, emit one coordinated cue, and keep at least one changed state observable in subsequent play.
accessibility: Caption environmental and musical transition cues
Offer reduced flash and camera-motion mode
Provide replayable factual before-and-after recap
Allow longer orientation pauses without changing state

failureModes: Performance cue disagrees with committed state
recovery: Atomic rollback to the before-state checkpoint
Recompute affected states from the transition fact
Route player to a stable comparison anchor

originality.retainedPrinciple: Treat a player-completed world-state transition as one atomic change: stage a coordinated before-and-after cue and make at least one affected navigation, actor, or interaction state observable in subsequent play.
forbiddenTransfers: source milestone cast and location sequence
source transition camera, music, and reward cadence

combinationVetoes.vetoWhen: World duality, restoration objective, affected cast, before/after topology, and transition cadence converge on one source title.

precedence.tieBreak: Select only for the opening contract when the first meaningful action simultaneously teaches control, role, and immediate goal.

scoreCriteria.rule: Reward actions that reveal who the player is through responsibility and consequence.
Require the action's result to establish a concrete next intention.
Reward low-cost retry and clear local feedback.
Reward meaningful input before explanatory dialogue accumulates.

hardVetoes: Opening action is a disconnected controls tutorial
Role or goal remains unknowable after the consequence
First failure can consume a required resource or branch
Opening reproduces a source rescue or item-recovery sequence

supports.supportedInvariant: An urgent opening can teach movement, entry, danger response, and player responsibility as one motivated action chain.
Recovering the means to act can simultaneously establish the player's vulnerable starting situation and exploration loop.

counter.challengedAssumption: A readable foundational verb automatically constitutes action-first onboarding.
counter.boundary: A versatile combat control principle can improve the whole game without proving that the opening action teaches player role, immediate need, and story direction.
counter.derivedAbstention: Use a verb-teaching or combat-readability pattern when the action does not carry opening narrative identity.

feedback.subject: immediate need
feedback.requirement: Show the affected person, object, or condition in the same local action space.

feedback.subject: first control
feedback.requirement: Prompt one verb only after the player faces its valid target.

feedback.subject: action consequence
feedback.requirement: Make every input change the urgent situation visibly or audibly.

feedback.subject: situated response
feedback.requirement: Use short in-world reactions that confirm responsibility without pausing control.

feedback.subject: consequence cue
feedback.requirement: Let an actor response or changed scene name the next direction after the player has earned context.

feedback.subject: role and goal recap
feedback.requirement: Record only facts demonstrated or explicitly communicated.

invariants: Every tutorial input serves story need
No opening failure consumes irreplaceable state

revealRules: Demonstrate before naming role facts
Do not disclose biography unrelated to the immediate action
interactionWindows: Meaningful control arrives before extended dialogue
Player may inspect after first consequence before proceeding
performance.cueIntent: Let identity arrive as responsibility felt in the hands.

topologyRequirements: Opening need and valid target share a compact safe space
First route has no ambiguous dead-end
Next direction becomes visible from resolved site
stage.blocking: Actors invite action through need, gaze, movement, or obstruction without issuing a multi-step lecture.
stage.camera: Frame player, need, and first valid target together before prompt.
navigationAssertions: Every failed step returns to current safe anchor
Opening completion exposes exactly one clear immediate direction plus optional inspection

teach.task: Present one urgent, readable target and a safe verb whose consequence is immediate.
teach.successEvidence: Player performs the verb and can state what changed.
teach.failureRecovery: Reset only the local target and repeat contextual prompt.

practice.task: Ask for the same verb once more while an actor or environment responds to the player's responsibility.
practice.failureRecovery: Use a short situated cue, not a controls panel.

variation.task: Apply the opening responsibility to a second target with a changed spatial relation.
variation.successEvidence: Player transfers control and role expectation.
variation.failureRecovery: Highlight target affordance after a delay.

combineOrReverse.task: Chain two already introduced verbs or force a brief retreat that still serves the immediate need.
combineOrReverse.successEvidence: Player understands the action loop rather than one button.
combineOrReverse.failureRecovery: Restart at the current chain step with prior steps preserved.

exam.task: Resolve or transform the immediate need and infer the next goal from the consequence.
exam.successEvidence: Player can name role, present situation, and next direction.
exam.failureRecovery: Offer an optional factual recap and reopen local inspection.

runtime.fallback: Use a compact three-node opening with contextual prompts, immediate state changes, and a post-action dialogue line that confirms role and next goal.
accessibility: Allow prompt persistence and input remapping
Offer no-fail or slowed opening timing
Caption urgent offscreen cues
Permit replayable role-and-goal recap after action

recovery: Reduce urgency without changing canon outcome
Provide optional factual recap after attempt

originality.retainedPrinciple: The opening should teach control, player role, and immediate story direction through one motivated chain of safe actions.
forbiddenTransfers: source storm rescue and castle approach
source first cave, mentor, or iconic item staging

combinationVetoes.vetoWhen: Opening emergency, route shape, first verb or item, mentor function, and next landmark align with one source title.

precedence.tieBreak: Select when connective space itself sustains traversal, resource, puzzle, encounter, and route decisions; use constrained vehicle when a lane network is the authored challenge.

scoreCriteria.rule: Reward connections that change risk, knowledge, resource, or approach rather than only distance.
Require traversal plus at least two of observation, manipulation, encounter, resource, stealth, or social play.
Reward readable alternate approaches with distinct tradeoffs.
Require recovery and narrative orientation nodes amid continuous challenge.

hardVetoes: Overworld is mostly empty travel between isolated stages
A single rail or lane determines all progress
Density destroys landmark readability or recovery
Region layout or encounter chain copies a source field

supports.supportedInvariant: A connective landform can gain a new traversal or hazard rule and become a renewed challenge rather than a neutral corridor.
Visible terrain and landmarks can let traversal itself become route planning, observation, and risk management.
Vertical entrances and exits can connect multiple world layers so travel decisions produce new approach possibilities.

counter.challengedAssumption: Every expanding overworld network is a dense connective challenge field.
counter.boundary: A linear network can gain destinations and branch decisions while remaining a constrained-route system rather than traversable challenge space between nodes.
counter.derivedAbstention: Select constrained-vehicle-route when movement is bound to authored lanes or rails and off-network adjacency is not playable.

constraints: At least three verb categories across set
Each cell has entry, outcome, and recovery state
Narrative orientation or resource reset defined

feedback.subject: regional hierarchy
feedback.requirement: Reveal one dominant landmark and at least two intermediate affordances without showing a solved route.

feedback.subject: recovery orientation
feedback.requirement: Give recovery nodes a distinct, non-source sound identity.

feedback.subject: cell consequence
feedback.requirement: Show how the chosen verb changes hazard, resource, route, information, or encounter state.

feedback.subject: new local option
feedback.requirement: Expose changed adjacency in the world before adding it to any map overlay.

feedback.subject: discovered connection
feedback.requirement: Record only traveled or directly observed links and preserve uncertainty elsewhere.

feedback.subject: recovery route
feedback.requirement: Offer optional orientation to a known recovery node without auto-solving the next challenge.

invariants: Every cluster has recovery
No single verb solves every connection
Travel-only cells remain below density ceiling

revealRules: Reveal approach categories before exact solutions
Use landmarks and consequences instead of objective trails
interactionWindows: Player may observe before committing to each cluster
Breathing nodes support optional story response without trapping movement
performance.cueIntent: Make crossing the world feel like continuous thinking and discovery, not downtime between content.

topologyRequirements: Connection graph contains loops or cross-links
Each cluster offers at least one alternate approach or bypass
Recovery node lies within bounded traversal cost
stage.blocking: Use terrain, actors, hazards, and props to express route tradeoffs before interaction.
stage.camera: Preserve dominant landmark orientation while letting local occlusion create discovery.
navigationAssertions: Every required destination reachable through at least one supported verb set
Every challenge failure reaches recovery
No alternate approach collapses into identical cost and outcome

assertions: At least 65 percent of connections carry a decision or consequence

teach.task: Build a compact region where a landmark, one challenge cell, and a nearby recovery node are simultaneously readable.
teach.successEvidence: Player crosses using one supported verb and can return safely.
teach.failureRecovery: Recovery node resets local hazard and reframes the landmark.

practice.task: Offer two connections to the same intermediate goal with distinct risk, resource, or information tradeoffs.
practice.successEvidence: Player makes and can explain a route choice.
practice.failureRecovery: Both approaches loop back to a known junction.

variation.task: Change one connective landform with a new local rule or world state on revisit.
variation.successEvidence: Player re-reads known topology instead of following a new corridor.
variation.failureRecovery: Stable landmark and one unchanged connection preserve orientation.

combineOrReverse.task: Link traversal, environmental manipulation, encounter avoidance, and resource recovery across a multi-cell loop, or enter a known cluster vertically from a new layer.
combineOrReverse.successEvidence: Player composes verbs and discovers a shortcut.
combineOrReverse.failureRecovery: Partial outcomes persist only when they cannot strand the player.

exam.task: Let the player author a route across a dense region toward a story goal while balancing risk, secrets, resources, and breathing opportunities.
exam.successEvidence: Multiple viable route traces reach the goal with meaningfully different costs.
exam.failureRecovery: Known recovery nodes and reversible cell transactions prevent dead ends.

runtime.fallback: Represent the region as an illustrated node graph with short playable challenge segments, explicit connection tradeoffs, persistent discoveries, and bounded recovery nodes.
accessibility: Offer optional landmark compass and traveled-path trace
Provide reduced encounter and traversal intensity
Never communicate route danger by color alone
Allow return-to-known-recovery without losing committed discoveries

recovery: Add loopback shortcut to known junction
Return to nearest verified recovery node

originality.retainedPrinciple: The overworld can be the primary challenge space when its connections continuously demand observation, traversal, resource, encounter, and route decisions.
forbiddenTransfers: source overworld geography and landmark placements
source climb-glide, sky-surface-depth, or revisited-province challenge chain
source enemy camps, shrines, towers, tracks, or field reward cadence

combinationVetoes.vetoWhen: Macro geography, landmark hierarchy, traversal verb set, secret distribution, and region-state progression converge on one source overworld.
